steps.title: Identify the surrounding structure
steps.content: To the left of the blank sits the adjective “African,” which needs a plural noun to modify. To the right follows the relative clause “who won elections” and the prepositional phrase “during the decade…,” so the blank must supply a plural noun that can be modified by that clause.

steps.title: Apply the noun‑phrase rule
steps.content:
Choice D gives the plural noun “Americans” without an apostrophe, allowing the relative clause “who won elections” to attach correctly.

The other three either insert an unnecessary apostrophe, turning the noun into a possessive, or use the singular form, which breaks the plural meaning required by “nearly two thousand.”
That matches choice D — D is correct.
